title: Scaffold pnpm workspace (root package.json, pnpm-workspace.yaml, tsconfig base)
acceptance: pnpm install resolves apps/* and packages/* as workspaces

title: Create packages/core with types.ts (TtsEngine/TtsInput/TtsOutput/TtsVoice)
acceptance: types importable from both apps/web and apps/api

title: Implement textSegmenter.ts (trim, collapse spaces, reject empty/too-long, chunk)
acceptance: unit tests cover empty, over-max, normalization, chunk boundaries

title: Define G2P abstraction in packages/core/g2p (GPL-free default: HeadTTS CMU+NRL or misaki-EN fallback=None; espeak-ng only as opt-in plugin for non-English)
acceptance: phonemizer pluggable; English path has zero espeak-ng/GPL dependency; espeak-ng isolated as user-installed plugin, never bundled in core; license note recorded per docs/LICENSING.md

title: Implement audioUtils.ts PCM(float32)->WAV encoder + duration
acceptance: encodes valid 24kHz mono WAV playable in browser; unit test on header bytes

title: Vite + TS browser app skeleton (apps/web) with PRD §14 layout
acceptance: app loads; header, text input, voice/engine selector, generate, player, download, error region render

title: Browser Kokoro adapter via kokoro-js / onnxruntime-web (WebGPU with WASM fallback)
acceptance: model loads with progress; WASM used when WebGPU absent

title: Wire generate flow: text -> segment -> G2P -> synthesize -> preview -> download
acceptance: user generates audio fully in browser, no backend; plays and downloads

title: Browser states & errors: loading, busy, readable error messages
acceptance: model-load failure and generation failure show readable messages; no permanent freeze on large text

title: Accessibility + mobile responsive pass (keyboard nav, labels, visible loading)
acceptance: keyboard-only flow works; screen-reader labels present; usable on mobile width

title: Browser tests (Playwright, tiny mock/fixture model)
acceptance: tests: app loads, input works, generate toggles busy, player appears, error on model fail

title: Set COOP/COEP cross-origin-isolation headers (SharedArrayBuffer for multithreaded WASM)
acceptance: dev (vite-plugin-cross-origin-isolation) and prod return COEP:require-corp + COOP:same-origin; Web Worker WASM mode works; documented in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md

title: Kokoro naturalness tuning (reduce robotic feel): default FP16 not Q4, expose natural voices (af_heart/af_bella/am_michael), feed segmentText-chunked text for prosody
acceptance: quality path defaults to FP16; natural voices selectable; long text chunked via @local-tts/core segmentText with punctuation preserved; audible A/B improvement vs Q4+default voice; see docs/ENGINES.md Tier 1

title: Fastify + TS API skeleton (apps/api) with config loader from env
acceptance: server boots; reads all TTS_* env vars; config unit-tested

title: Engine registry + Node Kokoro adapter (onnxruntime-node), idempotent load()
acceptance: engine loads model once; registry exposes license metadata

title: worker_threads inference pool + bounded queue (avoid event-loop block)
acceptance: long synthesis does not block /health; saturated queue rejects with GENERATION_FAILED 503

title: POST /api/tts with validation (empty, max length, format) returning audio/wav
acceptance: returns WAV; rejects empty/too-long/unsupported-format with JSON error codes

title: GET /api/voices and GET /api/engines
acceptance: voices return id/name/language/engine; engines return status + license + commercialUse

title: GET /health (status, engine, modelLoaded)
acceptance: returns ok once default engine loaded; degraded on load failure

title: Error format + safe errors (no filesystem paths leaked) + configurable CORS
acceptance: all errors JSON per PRD §15; no path/secret leakage; CORS honors env

title: API tests (health/voices/engines/tts + rejection cases)
acceptance: all PRD §19.2 cases pass

title: Dockerfile (node:20-slim): install deps, copy app, expose port, start API
acceptance: image builds; container starts API

title: Docker HEALTHCHECK calling /health
acceptance: container reports healthy after model load

title: docker-compose.yml: ports, env, models volume (output volume optional/off by default)
acceptance: docker compose up --build serves http://localhost:3000; models mountable

title: Optional model preloading + setup script for model download
acceptance: first run can preload or mount local models; documented in README

title: Docker smoke test: compose up then curl /health == {status:ok}
acceptance: PRD §19.4 passes

title: Piper ONNX Node adapter + per-voice license.json metadata
acceptance: engine switch to piper works; each voice has verified license metadata

title: Engine switching via TTS_ENGINE without rewriting API or UI
acceptance: changing default engine needs only config; UI/API unchanged

title: Optional Chatterbox PyTorch sidecar + thin Node HTTP adapter
acceptance: chatterbox adapter calls sidecar; documented as separate service, GPU optional

title: Orpheus TTS GPU sidecar engine (Apache-2.0, Llama-based, emotive) reusing the Chatterbox sidecar pattern
acceptance: orpheus adapter calls Python sidecar over HTTP; emotion tags supported; license.json present; engine selectable via TTS_ENGINE; see docs/ENGINES.md Tier 2

title: Add root LICENSE (MIT)
acceptance: root LICENSE present; app code MIT, models/voices/phonemizers keep own licenses (noted in LICENSE + docs/LICENSING.md)

title: Keep README/docs/task.jsonl in sync as phases land
acceptance: docs reflect shipped reality; DoD checklist in PRD §23 tracked

title: Per-engine license.json metadata files (engines/<id>/license.json)
acceptance: each registered engine ships license.json per docs/LICENSING.md; surfaced by GET /api/engines

title: Segmenter: handle abbreviations / decimals (e.g. 'Dr.', '3.14') to avoid mis-splits
acceptance: segmentText does not split on common abbreviations or decimal points; unit tests added

title: Piper ZH: improve voice-dropdown discoverability (group by language, don't bury non-EN voices at bottom)
acceptance: Chinese voices visible near top of dropdown when user types Chinese text; language label clear

title: Kokoro ZH: track kokoro-js releases for ZH voice-pack support; when available, add zf_* voices to the browser UI
acceptance: kokoro-js exposes zh-CN voices; UI shows them; Chinese text synthesizes via Kokoro (GPL-free, 4★ quality)

title: Language auto-detect: route ZH text to best available engine (Piper ZH → Kokoro ZH → Tier 2 sidecar) without user manually switching
acceptance: Chinese input auto-selects Chinese-capable engine; user can still override manually

title: Tier 2 ZH: Chatterbox/Orpheus GPU sidecar for Mandarin (5★, MIT/Apache-2.0, no GPL) — obsoletes Piper ZH path
acceptance: Chinese TTS via sidecar returns human-level audio; Piper ZH path deprecated; zero GPLv3 in ZH pipeline
